scope: Phase 3 domain-specific capability audit: high-risk secrets/trust authority slice plus cross-domain status inventory.

checks.note: Client RPC KV secret reads now map to domain-specific SecretsRead.
Transit encrypt uses TransitEncrypt with mount-scoped key name.
PKI issuance uses PkiIssue with mount-scoped role.
Standalone SecretsRequest mapping matches domain-specific ClientRpcRequest mapping.
Secrets capabilities are distinct from generic data capabilities.
Transit capabilities are distinct from generic data capabilities.
PKI capabilities are distinct from generic data capabilities.
SNIX CI endpoints already use SnixRead/SnixWrite for store authority.
Federation pull/push endpoints already use federation-specific operations.
Deploy control-plane endpoints map to ClusterAdmin.

finding: Concrete bypass class fixed for secrets/trust: ClientRpcRequest and standalone SecretsRequest mappings no longer authorize secrets, transit, PKI, or Nix-cache signing operations through generic _secrets: Read/Write prefixes. Generic data capabilities do not authorize the new domain-specific operations; Secrets/Transit/PKI capabilities do.
remaining_scope: Forge local repository operations, jobs/CI metadata, docs/hooks/blob/observability/net/coordination/automerge domain APIs still have generic internal-prefix mappings or no dedicated Operation variants; retain the umbrella task open for those follow-up slices.
